summary: A multidimensional supply shock is weakening the U.S. economy, fueled by the delayed effects of the 2025 trade wars and tariffs, elevated oil prices, and persistent policy uncertainty. NAHB Chief Economist Dr. Robert Dietz provides a high-level summary of key economic markers.

title: HBAs Celebrated for Member Growth and Retention with Grand Awards
summary: The latest Grand Awards winners include 22 local associations and 10 state associations.

summary: Wesley “Wess” Galyon, who served as president and CEO of the Wichita Area Builders Association for forty years, passed away.

title: Builder Sentiment Posts Notable Decline on Economic Uncertainty
summary: Builder confidence in the market for newly built single-family homes fell four points to 34 in April, according to the NAHB/Wells Fargo Housing Market Index (HMI) released today. This is the lowest level since September 2025.

title: Canadian Lumber Duties Expected to Drop This Summer
summary: The U.S. Department of Commerce has signaled that it plans to lower antidumping and countervailing tariffs later this year on imports of Canadian softwood lumber products from the current rate of about 35.16% to 24.83% following its annual review of existing tariffs.

title: 3 Key Reasons to Become a Speaker at IBS 2027
summary: What if one speaking opportunity could elevate your reputation, expand your network and put your ideas in front of thousands of the people shaping the future of home building? That’s exactly what the NAHB International Builders’ Show® (IBS) offers.

title: White House Economic Report Cites NAHB Regulatory Housing Studies
summary: The White House today released its Economic Report of the President (ERP) that contains a section on housing and homeownership that cites NAHB regulatory studies.

title: New York Builders Win Legal Challenge on Onerous Wetlands Rule
summary: In an important win for New York home builders and housing affordability, the New York State Supreme Court has issued a decision annulling the state’s new definition of what a “freshwater wetland” is based on violations of the State Environmental Quality Review Act (SEQRA).

title: Podcast: Housing Market Braces for Tense Spring Home Buying Season
summary: In the latest episode of NAHB’s podcast, Housing Developments, CEO Jim Tobin and COO Paul Lopez delve into market uncertainties ahead of the spring home buying season, efforts to bolster housing supply, what the 2027 budget cuts could mean for housing and how members can engage in the codes process.

title: Home Remodeling Profit Margin Jumps on Demand and Business Practices
summary: Profitability for residential remodelers reached its highest level in nearly 30 years in 2024, according to NAHB’s most recent Remodelers’ Cost of Doing Business Study.

title: The States and Construction Trades Most Reliant on Immigrant Workers
summary: Immigrant work totaled 26.3% of the entire construction workforce in 2024, a record high. The number is even higher among construction trades.

title: 5 Signals Many Home Builders Are Misreading Right Now
summary: Price cuts. Slower sales. Rising inventory. Elevated rates. Each of them is real. Each can be measured, but each can be easily misunderstood. For builders, it's critical how they interpret what’s happening and how they respond.

title: Remodeling Market Sentiment Edges Down but Remains Positive in First Quarter

title: Watch Livestream of Virtual Spring Board of Directors Meeting

title: Tuition-Free Alabama Trades Academy Backed by Key Community Support

title: ICC Public Comment Hearings on Proposed Building Code Changes Begin April 19

title: Trump Seeks Nearly $11 Billion Cut to HUD Programs

The spending reductions include a $10.7 billion cut — about 13% — for the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD).

title: Emerging Green Building Professional Spotlights Innovative Takeaways from IBS

This year’s winner is Grace Weger, a green builder making a meaningful impact in the world of affordable housing.

title: Construction Helps Lead Job Growth in March
